title: Bijapur Fort
tagline: Bijapur Fort — heritage city of Chhattisgarh

overview.short: The Bijapur Fort (Vijapur kote) is located in the Bijapur city in Bijapur District of the Indian state of Karnataka.
overview.description: The Bijapur Fort (Vijapur kote) is located in the Bijapur city in Bijapur District of the Indian state of Karnataka. Bijapur fort has a plethora of historical monuments of architectural importance built during the rule of Adil Shahi dynasty. The Adil Shahi Sultans who ruled for nearly 200 years in Bijapur had expended their utmost authority, almost exclusively, on architecture and the allied arts, each Sultan endeavored to excel his predecessor in the number, size, or splendor of his building projects. topPlaces.name: Bijapur
topPlaces.description: Bijapur (officially Vijayapura) is the district headquarters of the Bijapur district of the Karnataka state of India. It is also the headquarters for Bijapur Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Jod Gumbaz
topPlaces.description: Jod Gumbaz is a complex of two tombs located in Bijapur, in the Indian state of Karnataka. Built during the late Adil Shahi period and completed in 1687, it contains the tombs of Khan Muhammad and his spiritual advisor Abdul Razzaq Qadiri.

topPlaces.name: Malik-E-Maidan
topPlaces.description: The Malik-E-Maidan also Malik-i-Maidan (lit. 'Lord of the Battlefield'), is a 16th-century cannon, located at Burj-E-Sherz (Lion Tower), Bijapur Fort, Bijapur, India. At 4.45 metres (14.6 ft) in length and cast in bell metal, it is the largest surviving piece of artillery from the medieval period.

topPlaces.name: Ibrahim Rauza
topPlaces.description: The Ibrahim Rauza, also known as the Ibrahim Rawza, (lit. 'Ibrahim's tomb') is a funerary complex featuring a mausoleum, a mosque and extensive gardens, located in Bijapur (now Vijayapura), in the state of Karnataka, India. The buildings are very similar in style, with a fountain between them.

topPlaces.name: Bara Kaman
topPlaces.description: Bara Kaman is the unfinished mausoleum of Ali Adil Shah II in Bijapur, Karnataka in India. Ali Adil Shah of the Adil Shahi dynasty wanted to build a mausoleum of unmatched architectural quality.

topPlaces.name: Andu Masjid
topPlaces.description: The Andu Masjid, also known as Anda Masjid, is a mosque located in Bijapur, in the state of Karnataka, India. The mosque is a Monument of National Importance, administered by the Archaeological Survey of India.

topPlaces.name: Mecca Masjid, Bijapur
topPlaces.description: The Mecca Masjid, also known as Makka Masjid, is a mosque located in the Vijayapura district of Bijapur, in the state of Karnataka, India. Believed to be completed in c. 1669, the mosque was built for women of the royal household.

topPlaces.name: Nav Gumbaz
topPlaces.description: The Nav Gumbaz (literally "nine domes"), also called the Nau Gumbaz, is a mosque located in Bijapur, in the state of Karnataka, India. It was built during the reign of the Bijapur Sultanate.

items.note: Bijapur (officially Vijayapura) is the district headquarters of the Bijapur district of the Karnataka state of India. It is also the headquarters for Bijapur Taluk.

Jod Gumbaz is a complex of two tombs located in Bijapur, in the Indian state of Karnataka. Built during the late Adil Shahi period and completed in 1687, it contains the tombs of Khan Muhammad and his spiritual advisor Abdul Razzaq Qadiri.

The Malik-E-Maidan also Malik-i-Maidan (lit. 'Lord of the Battlefield'), is a 16th-century cannon, located at Burj-E-Sherz (Lion Tower), Bijapur Fort, Bijapur, India. At 4.45 metres (14.6 ft) in length and cast in bell metal, it is the largest surviving piece of artillery from the medieval period.

The Ibrahim Rauza, also known as the Ibrahim Rawza, (lit. 'Ibrahim's tomb') is a funerary complex featuring a mausoleum, a mosque and extensive gardens, located in Bijapur (now Vijayapura), in the state of Karnataka, India. The buildings are very similar in style, with a fountain between them.
Bara Kaman is the unfinished mausoleum of Ali Adil Shah II in Bijapur, Karnataka in India. Ali Adil Shah of the Adil Shahi dynasty wanted to build a mausoleum of unmatched architectural quality.

The Andu Masjid, also known as Anda Masjid, is a mosque located in Bijapur, in the state of Karnataka, India. The mosque is a Monument of National Importance, administered by the Archaeological Survey of India.

The Mecca Masjid, also known as Makka Masjid, is a mosque located in the Vijayapura district of Bijapur, in the state of Karnataka, India. Believed to be completed in c. 1669, the mosque was built for women of the royal household.
The Nav Gumbaz (literally "nine domes"), also called the Nau Gumbaz, is a mosque located in Bijapur, in the state of Karnataka, India. It was built during the reign of the Bijapur Sultanate.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bijapur Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bijapur Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bijapur Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kolhapur Airport (152 km) and the nearest railway station is Solapur Junction (96 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bijapur Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bijapur Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bijapur Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Bijapur Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bijapur Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bijapur, Jod Gumbaz, Malik-E-Maidan, Ibrahim Rauza, Bara Kaman.
